free_text: Spallation of vanadium induced by 1-GeV C-12 ions and
BY 258-, 350- and 910-MeV He-3 ions.
ISOLDE-cooperation.
Department of chemistry.
All decay data.
S B
Last checking has been done.
See sample.
The irradiations lasted one to two hours with
beam intensities of 0.13 - 1.92 microamperes.
Each sample was measured several times during
about one year.
The foil thicknesses were determined to within about
2% by weighing an accurately measured area of each
foil and were about 15-mg/cm**2-vanadium, 25-mg/cm**2-
carbon and 4-mg/cm**2-aluminium. The carbon catchers
used were spectrally pure with a main impurity of less
or equal 0.1-ppm Si.
.Monte-Carlo cascade evaporation calculations.
The recoil data have been analyzed with the two step
vector model to deduce the mean kinetic parameters.
Thick target/thick catcher recoil data for
radionuclides produced in the interaction of vanadium
with 258-, 350- and 910-MeV He-3-ions and 1032-MeV C-12
ions.
The errors listed are standard deviations based on
spread among parallels and the combined errors from
single bombardments due to counting statistics,
relative photopeak efficiency, corrections for recoil
losses and errors in thickness measurements for target
and monitor foils. For cross sections obtained, using
chemical separation, uncertainties in chemical yield
determination were also included. Systematic errors in
the monitor cross section are not included.
.The error is reported by authors in table.
Table 1 in paper

A vanadium foil, sandwiched between six carbon foils,
serving as guard foils, blanks, monitor and forward ans
backward catcher foils, respectively. The leading edge
was carefully cut before the irradiation to ensure
alignment of the different foils.
In two irradiations at 350-MeV He-3 was
determined the transtarget formation of Mn-51, Mn-52
and Mn-52-m by chemical separation.
The results which were obtained after radiochemical
separation were corrected for chemical yield.
